Watch: Is the ‘Hindi majority’ claim a statistical illusion?
description: Chapter 4 of Part I of the Report of the High-Level Committee on Union-State Relations deals with perhaps Tamil Nadu’s most sensitive issue — language. But this is not Tamil versus Hindi.  This is about what the Constitution actually says, whether Hindi numbers are inflated, whether English needs protection and whether India’s language policy needs a reset. It also challenges three assumptions: that Hindi is spoken by most Indians; migration makes Hindi essential, and the Three-Language Formula is working.

title: NCERT under scrutiny: Legal literacy or political agenda?

The project integrates with existing metro networks and promises direct airport connectivity, easing NCR commutes and strengthening economic ties.

Why Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift have gone quiet together
description: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift have quietly stepped away from public view, choosing private time over constant appearances. Kelce revealed they recently watched the Olympic hockey final together and shared family moments during a snowstorm on the East Coast. Their absence from public outings since January has fueled curiosity, especially with their wedding approaching. The details suggest not distance, but a deliberate effort to protect their relationship and enjoy ordinary life away from cameras.

title: 'Windpipes severed': Delhi woman and 3 daughters murdered in brutal attack
description: In a horrifying case from Outer Delhi, a 30-year-old woman and her three young daughters were found dead in their residence, their throats brutally slit. Investigators have identified the husband, a vegetable seller who has gone missing, as the primary suspect. Authorities are probing whether sedatives were administered before the killings, with forensic experts analyzing food samples for clues.

title: Ron Duguay reveals stage IV cancer battle, family launches GoFundMe
description: Ron Duguay, a former New York Rangers star now dating Sarah Palin, is battling Stage IV colon cancer. His daughters launched a $26,000 GoFundMe to help cover treatment and travel costs. According to Page Six, he has been fighting the disease for a year and has undergone major surgeries while continuing chemotherapy and other therapies.

title: White House negotiating with Google, Microsoft to cut power bills
description: Tech giants like Google, Microsoft, and Meta are set to pledge covering their own energy costs for new data centers. This White House initiative aims to prevent rising electricity bills for American consumers, driven by AI's massive power demands. Companies will invest in their own power solutions, avoiding grid strain and public backlash.

title: Maoist leader kills fellow cadre to stop surrender, buries body in Odisha forest
description: A Maoist leader allegedly murdered a fellow cadre in Kandhamal to prevent his surrender, with the body exhumed from a forest. This incident highlights efforts by senior leaders to deter cadres from laying down arms. The development follows a recent encounter where security forces killed two Maoists, as authorities intensify operations to meet the March 31 deadline to eradicate Naxalism.

title: Why this mechanical engineer from Bihar chose to become a househusband?
description: Amit Kumar Dubey, a proud house husband from Bihar, has redefined traditional gender roles by dedicating himself to full-time household management. He gave up his job to support his wife, handling cooking, cleaning, and childcare. Despite occasional worries about his responsibilities as the eldest son, Amit finds fulfillment in his role, supported by his wife and family.

title: The British Crown 'owes' Rs 10,00,00,000 to this Sehore family; heirs mull payback
description: A Sehore family is seeking to recover Rs 35,000 advanced by their grandfather to the British government in 1917. The prominent businessman's family claims the loan, made to the political agent in Bhopal, was never repaid. They are now exploring legal avenues to pursue this colonial-era financial claim against the United Kingdom.
